Title: Manual Linux availability-group failover
StatePreconditions: All replicas report the intended baseline role and synchronization state before the transition. The management network remains reachable independently of the data path.
AssertionBoundary: Assert role and synchronization-state transitions, not failover duration or an exact queue value.

Title: Availability-group suspend queue and seeding error
StatePreconditions: The availability group is healthy before the selected replica state is changed. The selected replica and database are bound to the active run ID.
AssertionBoundary: Assert the reported suspended, queued, or seeding state and source status; do not assert exact queue growth.

Title: Log Shipping backup copy and restore lag
StatePreconditions: The synthetic primary and secondary databases have an initialized log-shipping chain. The backup, copy, and restore metadata is scoped to the active run.
AssertionBoundary: Assert ordered backup, copy, and restore evidence with a visible lag state; do not assert a fixed elapsed time.

Title: Interrupted Log Shipping transfer
StatePreconditions: A healthy synthetic log-shipping cycle has completed before the transfer path is interrupted. The management path is outside the faulted data segment.
AssertionBoundary: Assert the interrupted transfer or delayed copy/restore state and recovery after fault removal; do not assert exact retry counts.

Title: Transactional-replication backlog
StatePreconditions: Publisher, distributor, and subscriber report a healthy synthetic baseline. The publication, subscription, and agents are run-owned.
AssertionBoundary: Assert backlog and agent-state evidence without asserting an exact command count or delivery rate.

Title: Stopped replication agent
StatePreconditions: The selected synthetic replication agent has completed at least one healthy cycle. The job identifier is registered in the active run state.
AssertionBoundary: Assert the stopped or failed agent state and related backlog visibility; do not infer root cause from job state alone.

Title: Unavailable subscriber
StatePreconditions: The subscriber has acknowledged a healthy baseline before isolation. The management path remains reachable and the data-path rule is registered by run ID.
AssertionBoundary: Assert subscriber unavailability, agent error or backlog evidence, and recovery after removing the fault; do not assert a fixed timeout.

Title: Synthetic backup chain
StatePreconditions: The target directory is a run-owned bounded storage path. The synthetic database uses the declared recovery model and run marker.
AssertionBoundary: Assert full, differential, and log-chain relationships and metadata status; do not treat file throughput as a benchmark.

Title: Slow or unavailable backup target
StatePreconditions: The fault target is a small run-owned path that is not the image cache, system path, or framework path. A healthy synthetic backup completes before the target fault is enabled.
AssertionBoundary: Assert a bounded failure, timeout, or partial-evidence status and successful recovery; do not assert exact latency or I/O waits.

Title: Network latency jitter and bandwidth limit
StatePreconditions: The selected data segment and management segment are separate and registered. The baseline connection succeeds before the seeded fault profile is applied.
AssertionBoundary: Assert connection, timeout, queue, or wait-context changes and recovery; do not assert exact packet statistics or wait duration.

Title: Packet loss duplication and reordering
StatePreconditions: The data-path interface is run-owned and distinct from management. The seeded fault profile has a hard duration and rate ceiling.
AssertionBoundary: Assert a bounded degraded or failed SQL path and recovery after rule removal; do not assert exact loss, duplication, or reordering counts.

Title: Bounded network partition
StatePreconditions: The management path is proven outside the selected partition. An automatic expiry and an independent cleanup path are registered before the partition is applied.
AssertionBoundary: Assert only the selected relationship becomes unavailable and returns after cleanup; do not claim a physical host or site failure.

Title: DNS port and TLS failure
StatePreconditions: The healthy synthetic endpoint succeeds before one exact DNS, port, or TLS property is changed. The original configuration is captured in run state without persisting secrets.
AssertionBoundary: Assert the specific connection failure class and restoration; do not include certificate private material, credentials, or raw environment paths.

Title: Failed long and disabled SQL Agent job
StatePreconditions: All job names and schedules use the synthetic run token. No existing job is modified or selected by a partial name.
AssertionBoundary: Assert failed, long-running, and disabled job states separately; do not infer business impact from job status alone.

Title: Service Broker queue and transmission backlog
StatePreconditions: The synthetic broker service completes a healthy dialog before queue state changes. Queue, service, route, and contract identifiers are run-owned.
AssertionBoundary: Assert queue-disabled or transmission-backlog metadata without reading message bodies or asserting exact queue growth.

Title: Successful and interrupted distributed transaction
StatePreconditions: The successful synthetic distributed transaction completes before the interrupted path is tested. Both participants and the transaction marker are run-owned.
AssertionBoundary: Assert visible transaction, blocking, or failure-isolation evidence; do not claim full MSDTC platform coverage from one container mode.

Title: Linked Server success timeout and outage
StatePreconditions: The linked-server analyzer from OPS-005 must exist before runtime actions may be marked implemented. Any opt-in connectivity action must use an isolated run-owned remote endpoint.
AssertionBoundary: This contract does not promise a per-call timeout for sp_testlinkedserver and does not alter server timeout configuration.

Title: Synthetic maintenance operation state
StatePreconditions: The synthetic maintenance job and target object are registered by the active run ID. The baseline has no paused or active operation before the bounded state is created.
AssertionBoundary: Assert paused or active maintenance evidence and its cleanup; do not issue RESUME, ABORT, KILL, or change an existing job.
